summary.vn_index_trend: VN-Index broke the 1,825 support floor flagged in the Q2-2026 framework, closing at 1,838.90 (-1.53% w/w) after a volatile week that opened at 1,867.44 and printed a 1,871.09 high before capitulating to an intraday low of 1,798.05. The Thursday breakdown candle breached 1,825 and tagged 1,798.05 on closing-auction pressure; Friday's 1,838.90 close represents a tactical 40-point dead-cat bounce off the low but failed to reclaim the 1,850 pivot. A wide 73-point weekly range (1,798.05-1,871.09) replaced last week's indecisive near-doji with a decisive bearish-engulfing structure, shifting risk-reward toward the 1,775/1,800 band. Breadth deteriorated: 6 of 9 tracked sectors closed red, with cyclicals (Real Estate -3.18%, Oil & Gas -3.41%, Securities -1.82%) leading the decline and Technology (+3.88%), Construction (+2.11%), and Retail (+1.96%) the only green. Foreign net registered -35 bn VND, flagged as a synthetic estimate in the absence of real-time flow data. Tone: defensive, with rotation from cyclicals into growth.
key_themes: Vietnam tech re-rating continues: Technology led the tape at +3.88% on VinFast/FPT tailwinds, now extending into broader ICT narrative despite index drawdown
Power-sector governance overhang persists: PV Gas/EVN ecosystem role remains in flux following last week's detention event; Utilities drag extends
WTI regime shift: oil spiked from USD 79.36 to 90.54, compressing refiner crack spreads and weighing on Oil & Gas (-3.41%)
DXY capitulation to 100.07 (from 104.33) failed to transmit to VND: USD/VND firmed to 26,275 (+3% vs. last reference), indicating SBV-managed depreciation for export cushion
VND decoupling from DXY: SBV tolerating weaker VND despite USD weakness, deliberate policy to absorb post-EV-incentive current-account dynamic and WTI shock
EV incentive regime continues to support VinFast volume ramp: 50% registration-fee waiver through 2030 still in force
EVN balance-sheet repair: ~150,000 bn VND deposit redeployment continues to defuse SOE credit-complex drag
Gold safe-haven bid unwinding: -3.10% w/w to 4,337.10 reduces defensive allocation support for EM portfolios
BTC weakness (-4.51% to 60,922.67) pressures VinFast/FPT retail-investor base and risk-asset correlation channel
Foreign-flow rotation: directional bias from cyclicals into Tech aligns with post-VinFast-capital-raise narrative
Liquidity normalized across the quarter at ~1,100-1,800 bn VND/day (USD ~43-67m), consistent with Q2-2026 baseline; prior week-1 0.61 print was a unit error in the source feed and has been corrected
summary.macro_environment: SBV posture shifted to managed depreciation: USD/VND firmed to 26,275 (-0.06% w/w) but is now ~3% above the 25,499 reference, with DXY collapsing from 104.33 to 100.07 yet VND not strengthening — clear decoupling indicating SBV is using the band for export cushion and current-account absorption rather than appreciation. DXY sub-101 print is the year's most significant USD move, reflecting Fed expectations reset and term-premium compression. WTI at USD 90.54 caps net-importer inflation risk while pressuring downstream refiners (PLX/BSR) via lagged administrative pump-price passthroughs. Gold at 4,337.10 and BTC at 60,922.67 both declined, signaling risk-on rotation out of defensive hedges. Corporate-side catalysts from Q2-2026 week 1 (EVN deposit redeployment, EV fee-waiver extension) continue to defuse structural drags, but the WTI spike and DXY/VND divergence forced macro back into the driver's seat this week. Overall: stable FX regime with deliberate managed weakness, contained inflation, but increased two-sided risk from cross-asset volatility.

technical_levels.key_moving_averages: MA20 to be formally calibrated next week with a second weekly close; current 1,838.90 print sets the initial base. Weekly structure shifted from near-doji to bearish-engulfing.

forward_risks: Sustained foreign selling breaking 1,798 support toward 1,775/1,750 (200-day region)
WTI spike above USD 95 forcing SBV policy response and reigniting inflation complex
DXY mean-reversion above 101 forcing sharper VND band test given current 26,275 level
VND decoupling stress if SBV widens band unexpectedly or DXY reverses sharply
Second governance event in power complex extending Utilities weakness beyond current drag
Refiner margin compression persisting (PLX/BSR crack-spread pressure) if WTI stays above 90
Upstream services capex delay risk for PVD/PVS if WTI fails to hold above 90
BTC weakness deepening weighing on VinFast/FPT retail-investor base and risk-asset correlation
Gold safe-haven unwinding freeing flows for either risk-on re-engagement or further EM de-risking
Liquidity running ~1,100-1,800 bn VND/day in line with Q2-2026 baseline; prior data-feed unit error (0.61 bn VND) corrected in subsequent weeks
Foreign-flow data flagged as synthetic estimate (-35 bn VND) — directional read unreliable until market-confirmed
Idiosyncratic catalysts (margin calls, regulatory actions) layering on macro tape and amplifying two-sided risk
